seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in OpenAI Agents SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in OpenAI Agents SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Agents SDK, Context Window Management failures manifest as agents accumulating excessive conversation history, tool outputs, and handoff metadata within the Runner's state, quickly exceeding token limits during multi-turn interactions. This is exacerbated by the SDK's automatic state persistence across agent handoffs and retries, where untrimmed messages bloat the context fed to models like GPT-4o. Specialized agents in handoff chains compound the issue, each appending verbose outputs without truncation.

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-agent research workflow with 5 specialized agents
howItManifests.symptom: Runner state exceeds 100k tokens after 8-10 handoffs
howItManifests.impact: Agents hallucinate or fail with 'context_length_exceeded' errors

howItManifests.scenario: Customer support agent with conversation history
howItManifests.symptom: Previous 20+ customer turns included in every tool call
howItManifests.impact: $500+ in unnecessary token spend per conversation

howItManifests.scenario: Code generation agent with iterative refinement
howItManifests.symptom: Full conversation history + all code versions in context
howItManifests.impact: Model ignores recent instructions, generates wrong code

howItManifests.scenario: Data analysis pipeline with multiple chart generations
howItManifests.symptom: Every dataframe preview and chart description retained
howItManifests.impact: Final summary agent receives 80% noise, 20% relevant data

detection.name: Token Count Monitoring
detection.description: Track context length before each agent invocation using tiktoken
detection.code:
import tiktoken
enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model('gpt-4o')

def check_context_length(messages):
    token_count = sum(len(enc.encode(msg['content'])) for msg in messages)
    print(f'Current context: {token_count} tokens')
    return token_count > 120000

detection.name: Runner State Inspection
detection.description: Inspect Runner.step() inputs to detect history accumulation
detection.code:
from agents import Runner

def monitor_runner_state(runner: Runner):
    state = runner.get_state()
    msg_count = len(state['messages'])
    if msg_count > 50:
        print(f'⚠️  Message explosion: {msg_count} messages in state')
    return msg_count

detection.name: Handoff Context Bloat Detector
detection.description: Detect when handoff messages exceed 30% of total context
detection.code:
def detect_handoff_bloat(messages):
    handoff_markers = ['handoff_to', 'received_from', 'agent_transfer']
    handoff_tokens = 0
    total_tokens = 0
    
    for msg in messages:
        tokens = len(enc.encode(msg['content']))
        total_tokens += tokens
        if any(marker in msg['content'].lower() for marker in handoff_markers):
            handoff_tokens += tokens
    
    bloat_ratio = handoff_tokens / total_tokens
    return bloat_ratio > 0.3

fixes.name: Selective Message Pruning
fixes.description: Keep only last N turns + critical system/tool messages
fixes.code:
def prune_context(messages, max_messages=25):
    # Keep system messages and recent conversation
    system_msgs = [m for m in messages if m.get('role') == 'system']
    recent_msgs = messages[-max_messages:]
    return system_msgs + recent_msgs

fixes.name: Runner Context Truncator
fixes.description: Wrap Runner with automatic truncation before each step

fixes.name: Summary Compression
fixes.description: Compress old conversation into single summary message
fixes.code:
async def compress_history(messages):
    if len(messages) < 20:
        return messages
    
    history = messages[1:-10]  # Skip system/recent
    summary_prompt = f"Summarize this conversation in 200 words: {' '.join(m['content'] for m in history)}"
    
    summary = await client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': summary_prompt}]
    )
    
    return [messages[0]] + [{'role': 'system', 'content': f'Conversation summary: {summary.choices[0].message.content}'}] + messages[-10:]

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically detects context window exhaustion patterns by monitoring token counts, message accumulation rates, and handoff bloat across all OpenAI Agents SDK Runners. It injects as output guardrails on each agent, validating context health before consumption and triggering automatic truncation or summarization when thresholds are exceeded. Vex's agent-specific policies track per-agent context growth, preventing specialized agents from poisoning downstream handoffs with excessive state.

Integration requires adding VexGuardrail to each agent's output validator. Vex maintains rolling baselines of healthy context sizes per agent type, alerting when deviations exceed 2σ. Remediation runs automatically via Vex's context compression pipeline, preserving critical reasoning traces while eliminating noise. Tracing integrates natively with Agents SDK observability.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail
from agents import Agent, Runner

# Install: pip install openai-agents vex-sdk

research_agent = Agent(
    name="Researcher",
    instructions="...",
    output_guardrail=VexGuardrail(
        policy_id="ctx-window-openai-agents",
        max_tokens=120000,
        auto_compress=True,
        handoff_limit=0.25
    )
)

analysis_agent = Agent(
    name="Analyzer",
    instructions="...",
    output_guardrail=VexGuardrail("ctx-window-openai-agents")
)

# Vex auto-instruments all handoffs
runner = Runner([research_agent, analysis_agent])
result = runner.run("Analyze market trends")

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one line of VexGuardrail to eliminate context window failures forever: `pip install vex-sdk`
